General

BRSR Standards Decoded: What Indian Businesses Must Know in 2026

By Growlity Team • 5/21/2026

Indian businesses are entering a new era where sustainability reporting is no longer optional — it is strategic. With investors, regulators, global buyers, and financial institutions demanding greater transparency, BRSR Standards have become one of the most important frameworks shaping corporate accountability in India.

In 2026, companies are no longer asking whether they should align with sustainability reporting requirements. The real question is: How quickly can they adapt before competitors move ahead?

From SEBI mandates to ESG-driven investment decisions, the shift toward responsible business practices is accelerating rapidly. Businesses that understand and implement BRSR reporting effectively are gaining stronger investor confidence, better brand credibility, improved stakeholder trust, and access to global supply chains.

This comprehensive guide explains everything Indian businesses must know about BRSR Standards — including their meaning, principles, applicability, benefits, reporting structure, implementation roadmap, common challenges, and future impact on ESG compliance in India.

What Are BRSR Standards?

Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting (BRSR) is a disclosure framework introduced by the Securities and Exchange Board of India for listed companies in India. The framework helps businesses disclose their Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) performance in a structured and measurable manner.

BRSR replaced the earlier Business Responsibility Report (BRR) to create more standardized, transparent, and data-driven sustainability disclosures.

The objective of BRSR is simple:

  • Improve ESG transparency
  • Encourage responsible business conduct
  • Align Indian reporting with global sustainability expectations
  • Help investors make informed decisions
  • Promote long-term sustainable growth

Today, BRSR is becoming a critical part of corporate strategy rather than just a compliance exercise.

Why BRSR Standards Matter More in 2026

The importance of sustainability reporting has increased dramatically over the last few years. In 2026, several major business trends are making BRSR more relevant than ever:

1. ESG Is Influencing Investment Decisions

Investors are actively evaluating companies based on ESG performance. Businesses with strong sustainability disclosures are often perceived as lower-risk and future-ready.

2. Global Supply Chains Demand Transparency

International buyers increasingly expect suppliers to provide sustainability data. Indian exporters and manufacturers are now required to demonstrate ESG maturity to maintain partnerships.

3. Regulatory Expectations Are Expanding

Indian sustainability regulations are evolving rapidly. Businesses that proactively align with reporting standards can avoid future compliance pressure.

4. Consumers Prefer Responsible Brands

Modern consumers are more conscious about environmental and social impact. Transparent reporting helps businesses strengthen brand trust.

5. Sustainability Is Becoming a Competitive Advantage

Companies using ESG strategically are improving operational efficiency, attracting investors, and building stronger reputations.

Understanding the Structure of BRSR Reporting

The BRSR framework is divided into multiple sections that collectively evaluate a company’s sustainability performance.

Section A: General Disclosures

This section covers:

  • Company profile
  • Operations
  • Employee data
  • Product and service details
  • CSR initiatives
  • Market presence

Section B: Management and Process Disclosures

This focuses on governance structures and sustainability management processes, including:

  • ESG policies
  • Leadership accountability
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Risk management systems

Section C: Principle-Wise Performance Disclosures

This section evaluates performance against the 9 National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC) principles.

These principles cover areas such as:

  • Ethics and transparency
  • Employee well-being
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Human rights
  • Consumer value
  • Inclusive growth

The 9 Core Principles Behind BRSR Standards

Understanding the principles behind the framework is essential for effective implementation.

Principle 1: Ethics, Transparency, and Accountability

Businesses should operate ethically and maintain transparent governance practices.

Principle 2: Sustainable Products and Services

Companies must design products and services responsibly throughout their lifecycle.

Principle 3: Employee Well-Being

Organizations should prioritize fair treatment, safety, diversity, and employee development.

Principle 4: Stakeholder Responsiveness

Businesses should engage responsibly with all stakeholders.

Principle 5: Human Rights

Companies are expected to protect and respect human rights across operations and supply chains.

Principle 6: Environmental Protection

Organizations must reduce environmental impact and improve sustainability performance.

Principle 7: Responsible Public Policy Engagement

Businesses should engage in policy advocacy responsibly and ethically.

Principle 8: Inclusive Growth

Companies should contribute positively to communities and economic development.

Principle 9: Consumer Value

Organizations should provide safe, transparent, and sustainable value to customers.

Who Needs to Comply with BRSR?

Currently, the top listed entities by market capitalization in India are required to file BRSR reports under SEBI regulations.

However, the influence of BRSR extends beyond listed companies.

Businesses indirectly affected include:

  • MSMEs supplying to large corporations
  • Manufacturers exporting internationally
  • Vendors within ESG-driven supply chains
  • Companies seeking investment or funding
  • Organizations preparing for future ESG regulations

This is why even non-mandated companies are beginning to adopt sustainability reporting frameworks proactively.

Key Benefits of Implementing BRSR Standards

Improved Investor Confidence

Strong ESG disclosures help investors evaluate long-term business resilience.

Better Brand Reputation

Transparent sustainability reporting strengthens trust among customers, employees, and stakeholders.

Stronger Supply Chain Positioning

Global buyers increasingly prioritize ESG-compliant suppliers.

Enhanced Risk Management

BRSR reporting helps businesses identify operational, environmental, and governance risks early.

Increased Operational Efficiency

Tracking sustainability metrics often leads to better resource optimization and cost reduction.

Future Regulatory Readiness

Companies adopting BRSR early are better prepared for evolving compliance requirements.

Common Challenges Businesses Face with BRSR

Despite its advantages, many organizations struggle with implementation.

Data Collection Complexity

Collecting sustainability data across departments can be difficult without proper systems.

Lack of ESG Awareness

Many teams are still unfamiliar with ESG terminology and reporting requirements.

Supplier Data Gaps

Supply chain sustainability information is often incomplete or inconsistent.

Resource Constraints

Mid-sized businesses may lack dedicated ESG teams or reporting infrastructure.

Evolving Reporting Expectations

Sustainability frameworks continue to evolve, requiring continuous adaptation.

How Businesses Can Successfully Implement BRSR Standards

Build Leadership Commitment

Successful ESG implementation starts from top management alignment.

Conduct a Gap Assessment

Evaluate existing sustainability practices against BRSR requirements.

Create a Cross-Functional ESG Team

Collaboration between HR, operations, procurement, legal, and finance is essential.

Invest in ESG Data Systems

Technology can simplify reporting, monitoring, and audit readiness.

Train Internal Teams

Employee awareness is critical for accurate and effective reporting.

Engage Supply Chain Partners

Suppliers should also align with sustainability expectations.

Continuously Monitor Performance

BRSR should become part of long-term business strategy, not a once-a-year exercise.

How ESG Reporting Creates Business Value

A manufacturing company preparing for international expansion faced increasing ESG-related questions from global buyers. Although the organization had sustainability initiatives in place, reporting was fragmented and inconsistent.

After aligning operations with structured sustainability disclosures:

  • ESG data visibility improved significantly
  • Investor discussions became more transparent
  • Supplier engagement strengthened
  • Compliance preparation became easier
  • Global buyer confidence increased

The company eventually positioned sustainability reporting as a strategic differentiator rather than just a compliance requirement.

This reflects a broader shift happening across Indian industries in 2026.

The Future of BRSR Standards in India

The future of corporate sustainability reporting in India is moving toward:

  • Greater ESG accountability
  • Digitized sustainability disclosures
  • Supply chain transparency
  • Climate-related reporting integration
  • Global ESG alignment
  • Mandatory assurance requirements

Businesses that act early will gain a significant competitive advantage.

Organizations that delay ESG adoption may eventually face investor hesitation, supply chain pressure, and reputational risk.

Why Businesses Should Treat BRSR as a Growth Strategy

One of the biggest misconceptions about sustainability reporting is that it exists only for compliance.

In reality, BRSR can directly support:

  • Market expansion
  • Investor attraction
  • Brand differentiation
  • Operational efficiency
  • Risk reduction
  • Customer trust

The companies leading the next decade of business growth in India are likely to be the ones that combine profitability with transparency and sustainability.

Conclusion

BRSR Standards are redefining how Indian businesses measure, communicate, and improve sustainability performance.

In 2026, ESG reporting is no longer a side initiative handled only by compliance teams. It is becoming a core business function influencing investment, reputation, procurement, partnerships, and long-term growth.

Businesses that embrace BRSR strategically will not only strengthen compliance readiness but also unlock new opportunities in an increasingly sustainability-driven economy.

The real advantage lies not in simply reporting sustainability data — but in using that data to build smarter, stronger, and more resilient businesses.

FAQs

What are BRSR Standards?

BRSR Standards, or Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting Standards, are ESG disclosure guidelines introduced by the Securities and Exchange Board of India for listed companies in India. These standards help businesses report their environmental, social, and governance performance in a structured and transparent way. The framework is designed to improve accountability, strengthen investor confidence, and align Indian businesses with global sustainability expectations. In 2026, BRSR Standards are becoming a major benchmark for responsible corporate governance and sustainable business operations.

Who is required to comply with BRSR reporting in India?

Currently, BRSR reporting is mandatory for the top listed companies in India based on market capitalization under SEBI regulations. However, many non-listed companies, MSMEs, exporters, and supply chain partners are also voluntarily adopting BRSR practices because large corporations and global buyers increasingly expect ESG transparency across the value chain. Businesses preparing early for sustainability reporting are often better positioned for future compliance requirements and investor expectations.

Why are BRSR Standards important for businesses in 2026?

BRSR Standards are becoming increasingly important because sustainability performance now directly influences investment decisions, supply chain partnerships, brand reputation, and regulatory compliance. Investors are evaluating ESG risks more closely, and global customers prefer businesses that demonstrate responsible operations. In 2026, companies that effectively implement sustainability reporting frameworks are gaining a stronger competitive advantage, better stakeholder trust, and improved long-term resilience.

What are the key principles covered under BRSR?

BRSR reporting is based on the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). These principles focus on ethical governance, employee well-being, environmental protection, human rights, stakeholder engagement, sustainable products and services, inclusive growth, and responsible business practices. Together, these principles help organizations build a more transparent and sustainable operational framework.

How does BRSR reporting benefit investors and stakeholders?

BRSR reporting gives investors and stakeholders greater visibility into how a company manages sustainability risks and opportunities. Transparent ESG disclosures help stakeholders assess long-term business stability, governance quality, environmental impact, and social responsibility. This increased transparency often improves investor confidence, strengthens stakeholder relationships, and enhances corporate reputation in competitive markets.

Is BRSR aligned with global ESG frameworks?

Yes, BRSR Standards are designed to align closely with international sustainability reporting trends and ESG expectations. The framework incorporates globally recognized sustainability concepts while adapting them to the Indian business environment. This alignment helps Indian companies improve comparability with international ESG reporting standards and strengthens their position in global markets and supply chains.

Can BRSR become mandatory for more companies in the future?

The scope of sustainability reporting in India is expected to expand gradually as ESG regulations continue evolving. Many industry experts believe that BRSR-related disclosure expectations could eventually extend beyond the largest listed companies. Businesses that proactively adopt ESG reporting frameworks today are likely to be better prepared for future compliance requirements and market expectations.
